html:
Manure is an important resource for Farming. It is generated renewably by several creatures in the world, most notably Beefalo which make lots of it in herds for easy collection, but also including Koalefants, Pigs, and Splumonkeys. It can stack up to a maximum of 20 in the Inventory or on the ground and does not spoil over time.

Manure can be applied to Farm Soil Turf to add 8 Manure Nutrients. Manure can also be used to fertilize Grass Tufts or Berry Bushes after they have been dug up with a Shovel and replanted, although 1 Rot is just as effective for this. Guano works similarly, but adds 16 Manure Nutrients to Farm Soil Turf instead.

In a pinch, Manure can also be used as fuel in Campfire or Fire Pits. It will burn for 45/90 seconds and releases a gas cloud when burned, which is only a visual effect. For practical purposes however, Logs are much easier to collect.

Manure can be crafted by Wilson with 6 Rot after mastering "Transmute Icky III".

Gathering

Plop Patrons Method of Contribution
Periodically
Eating Veggies

(x1) (x2) With

Gathering methods

Manure can most easily be gathered near Beefalo herds, typically found in Savanna biomes. Each Beefalo (adult or baby) randomly drops Manure as they roam around the map. This can be helped by trapping the herd in a pen, but Beefalo can escape pens when in Heat.

Manure can also be generated by Pigs. When a Pig consumes a flower petal, vegetable or fruit food item (not meat which instead tames them), they will drop Manure. Pigs accept only one non-meat food per fifteen seconds. After this time has elapsed, the player can feed the Pig again. However, when a Pig becomes a Werepig, it will eat entire stacks of food one item after another and produce entire stacks of Manure at once. While more tricky, this makes them much more efficient.

Koalefants also drop Manure similar to Beefalo; however, as only one Koalefant spawns at a time, it is less efficient to gather from them. This can be done easier by trapping the Koalefant in a pen.

Splumonkeys will seek out Fruits and Vegetables on the ground and eat them to produce Manure in their inventory to throw at attackers, which inflicts damage and lowers sanity. The projectile will then drop on the ground and can be picked up. They will also seek out Manure on the ground to arm themselves. When killed they will drop all held Manure. When destroyed, Pig Torches and Splumonkey Pods drop Manure.

“That's one healthy bowel movement.”
–Wanda

Manure can be crafted by Wilson with 6 Rot after mastering "[[Transmute Icky III]]". == Gathering == {| border="0" cellpadding="1" cellspacing="1" class="wikitable" style="width: 500px;" |- ! scope="col"|Plop Patrons ! scope="col"|Method of Contribution |- |{{Pic|42|Beefalo}} {{Pic|43|Koalefant}} {{Pic|45|Winter Koalefant}} {{Pic|40|Ewecus}} |'''Periodically''' |- |{{Pic32|Pig}} {{Pic32|Werepig}} |'''Eating [[Food Type/DST#Veggies|Veggies]]''' |- |{{Pic|40|Splumonkey}} |'''Thrown''' |- |{{Pic|24|Pig Torch}} (x1) {{Pic|40|Monkey Totem|Splumonkey Pod}} (x2) |'''With {{Pic32|Hammer}}''' |} === Gathering methods === Manure can most easily be gathered near Beefalo herds, typically found in [[Savanna]] [[biome]]s. Each Beefalo (adult or baby) randomly drops Manure as they roam around the map. This can be helped by trapping the herd in a pen, but Beefalo can escape pens when in [[Beefalo Heat|Heat]]. Manure can also be generated by Pigs. When a [[Pig]] consumes a flower petal, vegetable or fruit food item (not meat which instead tames them), they will drop Manure. Pigs accept only one non-meat food per fifteen seconds. After this time has elapsed, the player can feed the Pig again. However, when a Pig becomes a [[Werepig]], it will eat entire stacks of food one item after another and produce entire stacks of Manure at once. While more tricky, this makes them much more efficient. Koalefants also drop Manure similar to Beefalo; however, as only one Koalefant spawns at a time, it is less efficient to gather from them. This can be done easier by trapping the Koalefant in a pen. Splumonkeys will seek out [[Fruits]] and [[Vegetables]] on the ground and eat them to produce Manure in their inventory to throw at attackers, which inflicts damage and lowers sanity. The projectile will then drop on the ground and can be picked up. They will also seek out Manure on the ground to arm themselves. When killed they will drop all held Manure. When destroyed, [[Pig Torch]]es and [[Splumonkey Pod]]s drop Manure. == {{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage == {{Recipe |item3 = Papyrus |count3 = 2 |item2 = Seeds |count2 = 5 |item1 = Manure/DST |count1 = 5 |tool = Science Machine |result = Horticulture, Abridged |character = Wickerbottom}} {{Recipe |item1 = Manure/DST |count1 = 3 |item2 = Bone Shards |count2 = 2 |item3 = Log |count3 = 4 |tool = Alchemy Engine |result = Bucket-o-poop}} {{Recipe |item1 = Manure/DST |count1 = 5 |item2 = Rot |count2 = 8 |item3 = Living Log |count3 = 2 |tool = Science Machine |result = Mushroom Planter}} {{Recipe |item1 = Manure/DST |result = Poop Pellets |resultcount = 20 |tool = Science Machine |character = Walter }} {{Recipe |item1 = Manure/DST |item2 = Cut Grass |tool = Terra Firma Tamper |result = Savanna Turf |resultcount = 4 }} {{Recipe |item2 = Mosquito Sack |count2 = 1 |item3 = Nitre |count3 = 2 |item1 = Manure |count1 = 1 |result = Fertilizzzer |character = Wurt |skill = Itchcraft I }} {{Recipe |item1 = Manure/DST |count1 = 2 |item2 = Boards |result = Festive Tree Planter |note = Only during [[Winter's Feast]] event}} =={{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Beefalo and Poop Pins.jpg|A Beefalo and Manure pin set.
